steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must complete the logical conclusion of the study. The study found "similar correlations between patterns of these factors and of personality traits" across 53 countries — meaning the personality-to-music-preference relationship holds consistently across cultures. The answer must follow directly from that finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — non-Western music prediction: the study only assessed preferences for Western music genres. Extending the framework to non-Western music goes beyond what the data supports. ✗
  • Choice B — "varies less than previously assumed": the study did not compare its results against prior assumptions or previous research. The phrase "previously assumed" is not grounded in anything the passage states. ✗
  • Choice C — familiarity drives stronger preferences: familiarity with Western music is never mentioned in the passage. This introduces a variable the study did not measure. ✗
  • Choice D — similar personality profiles predict similar musical preferences across cultures: this follows directly. If personality-factor correlations are similar across 53 countries, then people who share personality profiles will tend to share musical preferences regardless of their cultural context. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
